This article is about the store-bought Aghanim's Scepter. For Roshan's kill reward, see Aghanim's Blessing - Roshan.

Aghanim's Scepter is an item purchasable at the Base Shop, under Magical. However, it requires an item from the Secret Shop to be completed.

Aghanim's Scepter
The scepter of a wizard with demigod-like powers.
Magical
Cost 4200
Sell Value 2100
Passive ? Ability Upgrade
Bonus ? +10 Strength
+10 Agility
+10 Intelligence
+175 Health
+175 Mana
Shareable 0
Disassemble 0
Usage Alert ? 0
Recipe

Additional Information

[edit]
  • The item's attributes grant the following additional stats:
    • 220 health and 1 health regeneration.
    • 120 mana, 0.5 mana regeneration and 1% flat base magic resistance.
    • 10 attack speed and 1.67 main armor.
    • 10 main attack damage to all / / heroes.
    • 13.5 main attack damage to universal heroes.
  • Upgrading the item into Aghanim's Blessing automatically consumes the item, converting it into a permanent buff.
    • As a permanent buff, it provides the ability upgrades like the item, but does not provide attributes, health, or mana.
    • The total gold cost for each level is 4200/5800 .
  • Aghanim's Blessing is also a Kill Reward item guaranteed to drop on his third death and onward, when killed on the Dire side.

Ability

[edit]

Ability Upgrade

[edit]
Ability Upgrade
▶️
Ability
Passive
Affects
Self
Upgrades an existing ability or grants a new ability, unique to each hero.
  • Details
  • Interactions
  • Status Effects
  • Misc.
  • Show All
  • Grants each hero one or multiple unique enhancements, ranging from ability upgrades, to hero changes, and sometimes even new abilities.
  • Aghanim's Scepter status of allies can be seen in the scoreboard at all times.
    • Enemies' status can be seen as well, but it only updates once gaining vision over them.
Interactions: Self-Illusions
Illusions copy the modifier on their creation. The upgrades fully work for them.
Interactions: Ability Draft
Most upgrades work normally for the player that drafted the abilities. For exceptions, see Ability Draft.
Interactions: Spell Reflection
Reflected abilities are upgraded based on the original caster's upgrades.

Aghanim's Scepter & Shard UI element.
  • Has a dedicated interface element within the HUD, along with Aghanim's Shard. The symbol lights up once Aghanim's Scepter is acquired.
    • A tooltip pops up when pointing the mouse on this HUD element, showing the hero's ability upgrades.

Upgraded Abilities

[edit]
Main Article: Aghanim's Scepter/Upgraded Abilities

Recommended Heroes

[edit]

Others

[edit]
  • Alchemist has the ability to directly infuse Aghanim's Scepter without the need of the expensive recipe. Unlike other heroes, he can also infuse it into his allies, instead of only himself, saving the team a lot of gold the more scepters he infuses.
  • Lone Druid can have Spirit Bear attack without him around or alive, which make an effective and safe pusher who will always threaten the enemy base.
  • Nyx Assassin unlocks Burrow, increasing his regeneration, and helping him use more abilities in team fights and defending buildings.
  • Pangolier's Shield Crash casts a 2-attack Swashbuckle at every 90 degree angle around him, increasing his damage output.
  • Razor deals more overall damage with Eye of the Storm striking two targets at once; it also damages buildings so Razor can push and close down the game.

Supports

[edit]

While many supports become more powerful with Aghanim's Scepter, most should still focus primarily on buying support items, with Aghanim's Scepter as a luxury option if they have enough gold.

Trivia

[edit]
  • The item's internal name is ultimate scepter.
  • Aghanim's Scepter is named after Agahnim, one of the main villains in The Legend of Zelda: A Link to the Past.
  • Rubick is the son of Aghanim. He also possesses an imitation of Aghanim's Scepter.
    • With the exception of Dark Willow, Grimstroke, Razor, Snapfire and Void Spirit, heroes never mention Aghanim's name in their voice responses, simply referring to it as "The Scepter". The reason for this is unknown.
    • Riki and Lion are the only heroes who do not have any Scepter responses (excluding Io and Phoenix, who do not speak).
  • The components of Aghanim's Scepter before version 6.60 are the same as the components of the Pre-7.27 Octarine Core.
